macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stock Markets Fall Amid Iran War Uncertainty and Rising Oil Prices
items.summary: Major US stock indexes declined on Monday as oil prices surged past $100 per barrel due to uncertainty about the Iran conflict. The S&P 500 fell 0.4% to 6,343.72, the Nasdaq dropped 0.7% to 20,794.64, while the Dow edged up 0.1% to 45,216.14. Year-to-date, the S&P 500 is down 7.3%, the Nasdaq down 10.5%.

items.title: Oil Prices Reach Highest Levels Since July 2022
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate crude surged 3.25% to $102.88 per barrel, its highest close since July 2022, while Brent crude rose to $112.78 per barrel. Oil is on track for a 55% monthly increase amid geopolitical tensions.

items.title: Federal Reserve Chair Powell Signals Inflation Expectations Remain Anchored
items.summary: Fed Chair Jerome Powell stated that inflation expectations remain 'well anchored beyond the short term' and that the central bank is not yet facing decisions on policy changes. The 10-year Treasury yield fell 9 basis points to 4.35% following his comments.

items.title: Blackstone Closes Record $6.3 Billion Life Sciences Fund
items.summary: Blackstone announced the final close of a record $6.3 billion life sciences fund, the largest private vehicle dedicated to the sector, signaling strong institutional demand for specialized allocations.

items.title: TJX Companies Announces 13% Dividend Increase
items.summary: The TJX Companies announced a 13% increase in common stock dividend on Monday.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Ethereum Price Rises to $2,070.90 with $70 Daily Gain
items.summary: Ethereum surged $70.70 to $2,070.90 by 8:45 a.m. ET on March 30, 2026, marking a notable increase from yesterday's $2,000.20 amid volatile yearly swings of over 80% gains and 60% drops.[1]

items.title: Bitcoin Climbs 1.33% to $67,334 on Institutional Accumulation
items.summary: Bitcoin rose 1.33% over the past 24 hours to $67,334 on March 30, 2026, driven by large wallets (1,000-10,000 BTC) adding $3.16 billion worth, signaling institutional interest despite regulatory hurdles from SEC custody guidance.[2]

items.title: Bitcoin Briefly Crashes Below $68K, Testing $65K Support
items.summary: Bitcoin dipped below $68,000 and tested mid-$65,000 levels before rebounding on March 30, 2026, with focus on March close after five straight monthly losses through February.[3]

items.title: Crypto Investors Urged to Ensure Tax Compliance Before March 31 Deadline
items.summary: As the March 31 financial year-end nears, crypto investors must follow structured tax compliance rules to avoid issues, with clear guidelines now established.[4]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Super Meat Boy 3D Launches Today on Xbox Game Pass and Multiple Platforms
items.summary: Super Meat Boy 3D, the full 3D evolution of the iconic hardcore platformer, releases on March 31, 2026, for Xbox Series X|S (day one on Game Pass), PS5, and PC. Announced during the Xbox Partner Preview, it promises precision platforming with high difficulty in 3D environments.[1][2]

items.title: Busy Wave of Game Releases on March 31 Including Tombwater and RACCOIN
items.summary: March 31, 2026, features multiple launches like atmospheric indie Tombwater, roguelike coin pusher RACCOIN, and racing expansion Project Motor Racing: Japanese GT500 Pack, highlighting indie variety and genre experimentation.[2][3]
items.source: DLCompare / YouTube Indie Gaming This Week

items.title: Legacy of Kain: Ascendance Releases Across Consoles and PC
items.summary: Legacy of Kain: Ascendance, a fast-paced 2D action game, launches on March 31, 2026, for PS5, Xbox Series X|S, Switch, Switch 2, and PC, reviving the classic series.[4]

items.title: Tom Clancy's The Division Activates Year 10 Content on March 31
items.summary: Ubisoft reveals activation date for Tom Clancy's The Division's 10th anniversary content on March 31, 2026, marking a major milestone for the looter-shooter franchise.[5]

items.title: Indie Highlights This Week: Xenonauts 2, Truck Mechanic, and More
items.summary: Indie releases from March 30-April 5 include Xenonauts 2, Truck Mechanic: Dangerous Paths, Voodoo Fishin', and others, with a big cluster on March 31 and potential April Fool's surprises.[3]

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Lumentum Announces New Laser Factory in Greensboro, North Carolina
items.summary: Lumentum, based in San Jose, California, will build a new manufacturing facility in Greensboro, North Carolina, to produce lasers for large AI computers. This is part of major US manufacturing expansions announced this week.

items.title: Tesla and SpaceX Launch Terafab AI Chip Factory Project in Austin, Texas
items.summary: Elon Musk's Tesla and SpaceX are developing Terafab, a massive factory in Austin, Texas, dedicated to manufacturing AI computer chips, highlighting cross-industry shifts into chip production.

items.title: Sysco Completes $2.9 Billion Acquisition of Jetro
items.summary: Sysco has sealed a $2.9 billion mega-deal to acquire Jetro, amid broader market movements including hedge fund pullbacks and rising geopolitical risks.

items.title: Analysts Recommend Uber, Palo Alto Networks, and Trimble as Top Tech Stocks for the Week
items.summary: Top analysts highlight Uber Technologies, Palo Alto Networks, and Trimble Inc. as the best tech stocks to buy this week ending March 30, 2026.
